title: HUD, Text, Menus, Focus, Multichannel Cues, and Motion Settings · Language Clinic: Focus Status
summary: Tiny centered text scales poorly, focus is color-only, menus require a mouse, damage flashes rapidly, status disappears immediately, and reduced motion removes essential information. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the focus status increment in accessible responsive game interface and settings flow. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Render persistent non-color focus and announce or retain important state, errors, destructive actions, and setting changes.
Support predictable keyboard and controller navigation, wrap policy, back, confirmation, cancellation, and no traps.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-hud-menus-accessibility-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-hud-menus-accessibility-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_hud_menus_accessibility_3(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Focus Status evidence · Theory Focus Status
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-focus-status-3bz4q3" that uses case 3602 to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Focus Status into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Focus Status evidence · Theory Focus Status, keep Focus Status connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-focus-status-3bz4q3
# Competency: asteroids-focus-status.
# Case 3602: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_focus_status_3bz4q3(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_3bz4q3 = "3bz4q3-3q4zb3-3602"
    assert evidence_variant_3bz4q3
    evidence_axes_3bz4q3 = (
        "player3bz4q3",
        "seed3602",
        "input3q4zb3",
        "frame3bz4q33602",
        "state36023bz4q3",
        "playtest3q4zb33602",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_3bz4q3)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Render persistent non-color focus and announce or retain important state, errors, destructive actions, and setting changes.
